good: Strong engagement rate (12.3%) and share count (238) shows topic resonates with MMA audience
Solid average watch time (8.4s) indicates viewers stay through majority of content
Bold text overlays create visual anchor points throughout video
Bilingual caption (Portuguese/English) expands potential audience reach
bad: Weak hook—frame 1 shows generic person reading, not the actual fighters; doesn't clearly communicate 'McGregor vs Holloway' until text appears
Very low saves (2) suggests content lacks reference/educational value viewers want to bookmark
Reaction commentary frames (2-4) feel repetitive; close-ups of the same person reacting becomes static after 3 frames
Missing fight footage or fighter imagery—pure reaction content underperforms vs. clips showing actual athletes/action
reels.top_tip: Replace frame 1 with split-screen or direct shot of McGregor and Holloway (photos or fight footage) to immediately communicate the matchup; reaction commentary works better as supporting layer, not the entire story

good: Clear UFC branding and professional octagon setting instantly establishes credibility
Text overlay provides fighter names and KO result context upfront without ambiguity

bad: Hook leads with fighting stance instead of the knockout impact itself—the most scroll-stopping moment comes later
Person recording/filming visible in Frame 1 background breaks professional production feel
Very low save count (3) despite exciting content suggests it's not triggering the 'save for later' impulse
Engagement rate 4.2% is soft for MMA knockout content which typically outperforms

reels.caption: 🇧🇷 E se o UFC soubesse que Conor McGregor já estava lesionado antes da luta? Cancelar significaria colocar em risco o ma
good: Exceptional share rate (17%) — McGregor conspiracy angle drives strong audience motivation to share the theory
Strong retention (17.1s watch time) — on-screen text progression creates narrative momentum that compels viewing
Face fills frame throughout with varied expressions — maintains visual attention without jarring cuts
Gesture in final frame breaks static compositions and adds visual punctuation
bad: Hook lacks visual punch — thinking face against plain dark background doesn't aggressively stop scrolls in competitive feed
Text-heavy hook requires Portuguese literacy; English-only viewers must read to understand context before engaging
Minimal saves (2) despite high shares — indicates entertainment/debate value without practical save-worthy utility
Low absolute reach (1,000) relative to engagement quality suggests algorithm isn't amplifying despite strong retention metrics

good: Exceptional engagement rate (56.7%) driven by debate-format content that encourages comments

High share-to-view ratio (122 shares from 731 views) indicates debate-worthy topic
Reaction format with clean visual hierarchy—face/reaction on top, fighter comparison below—is proven scroll-stopper
bad: Reach (393 accounts) significantly below views (731) indicates hook isn't optimized for cold viewers on explore page
Minimal save count (2) despite 56.7% engagement—content reads as debate fuel, not valuable/reference material worth saving
Mixed-language on-screen text (Portuguese/English transitions) creates inconsistency that confuses algorithm and non-Portuguese speakers
Mid-video portrait frame lacks visual dynamism—static close-up doesn't match momentum of earlier reaction shots

bad: No on-screen text overlay in hook frame — viewers cannot instantly decode topic before scrolling past
Hook leads with talking head rather than fight footage — weaker scroll-stopping power than typical UFC content
Extremely low saves (1 of 1142 views) indicates content lacks evergreen reference value despite strong shares
Truncated caption cuts mid-word 'princip...' requiring expand click, losing immediate narrative momentum

good: Clear text overlay with fighter names and UFC branding — instant context prevents confusion about what viewers are watching

Max Holloway vence Conor Mcgregor! Veja ai.

good: Clear matchup text in hook immediately establishes context and high-profile fighters

bad: Caption spoils the outcome ('vence' = 'beats') — kills curiosity and reduces shares/saves since there's no reason to send it

Also move the knockout moment to the first 1-2 seconds instead of mid-video.
